User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user registered with the Echoes application, managing family profiles and interacting with AI personas.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and notifications.

familyIds.description: References to Families this user belongs to or administers. (Relationship: User N:N Family)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Family.title: Family
Family.description: Represents a family unit, grouping related users and the memory profiles of their loved ones.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Family entity.

name.description: The name of the family (e.g., 'The Smith Family').

description.description: A brief description or motto for the family.

memberUserIds.description: References to Users who are members of this family. (Relationship: Family N:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the family record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the family record was last updated.

LovedOneProfile.title: LovedOneProfile
LovedOneProfile.description: Represents a deceased loved one, containing their personal details, a summary of their life, and the status of their AI persona.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LovedOneProfile entity.

familyId.description: Reference to the Family this loved one belongs to. (Relationship: Family 1:N LovedOneProfile)

firstName.description: The first name of the loved one.

lastName.description: The last name of the loved one.

birthDate.description: The birth date of the loved one.

deathDate.description: The death date of the loved one.

bio.description: A biographical summary or description of the loved one's life and personality.

aiPersonaStatus.description: The current processing status of the AI persona (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'ready', 'error').

voiceModelReference.description: An internal reference or identifier for the unique AI voice model generated for this loved one.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the loved one's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the loved one's profile was last updated.

MediaAsset.title: MediaAsset
MediaAsset.description: Represents a single piece of media (photo, video, audio, text) uploaded to build a loved one's memory profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaAsset entity.

lovedOneProfileId.description: Reference to the LovedOneProfile this media asset belongs to. (Relationship: LovedOneProfile 1:N MediaAsset)

uploaderUserId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this media asset. (Relationship: User 1:N MediaAsset)

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded media.

fileUrl.description: URL to the stored media file (e.g., in a cloud storage bucket).

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'audio/mp3').

fileSize.description: The size of the media file in bytes.

uploadDateTime.description: Timestamp when the media asset was uploaded.

description.description: A user-provided description or context for the media asset.

aiProcessingStatus.description: The status of AI processing for this media asset (e.g., 'uploaded', 'analyzing', 'analyzed', 'failed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the media asset record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the media asset record was last updated.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Records details of an interactive voice conversation between a user and an AI memory profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

lovedOneProfileId.description: Reference to the LovedOneProfile involved in this conversation. (Relationship: LovedOneProfile 1:N Conversation)

userId.description: Reference to the User who had this conversation. (Relationship: User 1:N Conversation)

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the conversation in seconds.

transcript.description: The full text transcript of the spoken conversation.

audioRecordingUrl.description: URL to the audio recording of the entire conversation.

summary.description: An AI-generated summary of the conversation's content (optional).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner user matching the {userId} wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Represents a family unit. This document contains a 'members' map (e.g., {uid: 'role'}) which is the source of truth for family membership and roles. All child entities will denormalize this map for authorization independence.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the family.

definition.description: Stores profiles of deceased loved ones within a specific family. Includes denormalized 'familyMembers' map (copied from parent /families/{familyId}) for authorization independence, allowing family members to access.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent family.

params.name: lovedOneProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the loved one's profile.

definition.description: Stores media assets uploaded for a specific loved one's profile. Includes denormalized 'familyMembers' map (copied from /families/{familyId}) and 'familyId' for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent family.

params.name: lovedOneProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent loved one's profile.

params.name: mediaAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the media asset.

definition.description: Stores transcripts and details of voice conversations with a loved one's AI persona. Includes denormalized 'familyMembers' map (copied from /families/{familyId}) and 'familyId' for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent family.

params.name: lovedOneProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent loved one's profile.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the conversation.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure for Echoes is designed with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and maintainability, strictly adhering to the mandated core design principles.Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):A cornerstone of this design is to achieve complete authorization independence. This means that Firestore security rules will never need to use get() operations to check permissions on parent documents. This is accomplished by denormalizing the authorization context from the Family entity down to all its child entities: LovedOneProfile, MediaAsset, and Conversation.Specifically, the Family document will contain a 'members' map (e.g., {'userId1': 'admin', 'userId2': 'member'}) which defines the roles of each user within that family. This entire 'members' map is then copied into every LovedOneProfile document, and subsequently into every MediaAsset and Conversation document associated with that LovedOneProfile and Family.For example, a MediaAsset document will include a 'familyMembers' field that mirrors the 'members' map of its parent Family. A security rule for a MediaAsset can then simply check request.auth.uid in resource.data.familyMembers.keys() to determine if the requesting user is authorized, without ever needing to fetch the parent LovedOneProfile or Family document. This dramatically simplifies security rules, makes them atomic, and improves performance by reducing document reads.Queryable by Authorization Posture (QAPs):The chosen structure inherently supports secure list operations. Because the 'familyMembers' map is denormalized into every relevant document, security rules can effectively filter documents at the database level. For instance, when a user attempts to list LovedOneProfiles, the rule can check allow list: if request.auth.uid in resource.data.familyMembers.keys(). Firestore's query-ability combined with this denormalization ensures that a user can only ever retrieve a list of documents for which they are explicitly authorized, making rules act as robust filters without application-level post-filtering.Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:User Profiles: users/{userId} represents private user data, accessible only by the owning user (via request.auth.uid == userId).Family & Loved Ones: families/{familyId} serves as the root for collaborative family data. All subcollections (lovedOneProfiles, mediaAssets, conversations) inherit their authorization from the Family document's 'members' map, ensuring consistent access control across related data.Hierarchical Paths: The structure /families/{familyId}/lovedOneProfiles/{lovedOneProfileId}/mediaAssets/{mediaAssetId} clearly defines ownership and relationships. Each level reinforces the context.Membership Map: The 'members' map on Family (and denormalized as 'familyMembers') provides a robust and explicit mechanism for managing collaborative access and roles within a family unit.Data Clarity and Predictability:Explicit State Modeling: Fields like 'aiPersonaStatus' and 'aiProcessingStatus' are used to represent the finite state of processing workflows, avoiding ambiguous boolean flags.Consistent Naming: Wildcards like {userId}, {familyId}, {lovedOneProfileId} are semantic and descriptive, enhancing debuggability and readability of rules.Radical Consistency: Authorization fields are consistently named ('familyMembers'), promoting a unified approach to security.This design strategy ensures a highly secure, scalable, and easily debuggable Firestore implementation, aligning perfectly with the core principles and mandates.
